purpose: Final candidate selection and production risk classification for 5 target scenes
legal_disclaimer: Production risk labels below are NOT legal advice. They reflect a layered risk assessment based on license text and the production variables of this project (dark_documentary color grade, film grain, Ken Burns motion effects applied to all images — all of which constitute derivative modifications under most CC BY-SA interpretations). SA clause analysis requires jurisdiction-specific legal counsel before commercial publication.

decisions.source_title: Hashima Island 2023 — from tourist vessel at sea
decisions.attribution_text: Hashima Island (2023) by ノーマルエディタ / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY-SA 4.0
decisions.risk_rationale: This production applies dark_documentary color grade, film grain, and very_slow_pan_right Ken Burns motion to all images. These modifications constitute derivative works under CC BY-SA. The resulting video incorporating this image would technically require CC BY-SA licensing, incompatible with standard commercial YouTube monetization. See license_notes.md Section 2.
decisions.shared_license_note: C001-A and C024-A are the same file (Hashima_Island_2023.jpg). A single legal clearance covers both S001 and S024. If cleared: one download, two crops.
decisions.fallback_source_title: Battle-Ship Island Nagasaki Japan — aerial exterior (CC BY 2.0)
decisions.fallback_notes: C001-C is aerial, not sea-level — minor visual mismatch for S001 opening. CC BY 2.0, no SA, legally clean. Same file already being downloaded for S007. No additional download needed.
decisions.paid_alternative: C001-D — PIXTA (軍艦島 海 全景). Royalty-Free, no SA. Recommended if CC BY-SA review takes >1 week.
decisions.exact_human_action: Option A (best visual): Submit license_notes.md Section 2 to legal counsel. Request written confirmation that CC BY-SA images used as color-graded B-roll in a commercial YouTube documentary do not require the whole video to adopt CC BY-SA licensing. If confirmed: download Hashima_Island_2023.jpg. One download covers S001 + S024. Option B (safe immediate fallback): C001-C is the same file already downloaded for S007 (Battle-Ship_Island_Nagasaki_Japan.jpg). Use it as S001 opening with aerial framing — legally clean, no additional download. Option C (clean paid): Purchase from PIXTA (軍艦島 海 全景 or Hashima sea exterior). Royalty-Free, no SA. Strongest commercial license.

decisions.source_title: Battle-Ship Island Nagasaki Japan — aerial, battleship silhouette
decisions.attribution_text: Battle-Ship Island Nagasaki Japan (2008) by kntrty / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY 2.0
decisions.risk_rationale: CC BY 2.0 has no ShareAlike clause. Attribution in video description is the only requirement. Color grading, film grain, and Ken Burns motion create no additional license obligation. Ready to download and use immediately.
decisions.companion_note: C007-B (Japan Government 1974 aerial, attribution-only, no SA) is historically significant — aerial of Hashima in the actual year of closure. Recommended as a 2-3 second ACT III insert during the 1974 narration. Same download destination (S007/). Same file also serves as C024-B for S024 final shot.
decisions.fallback_notes: C007-C is CC BY 2.0, same photographer, same session. Different angle, 1280x960. Use as alternate cut if two aerial angles are needed.
decisions.exact_human_action: IMMEDIATE — NO LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED. Download: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/6/6b/Battle-Ship_Island_Nagasaki_Japan.jpg. Save to: assets/real_images/downloaded/S007/Battle-Ship_Island_Nagasaki_Japan.jpg. Create companion Battle-Ship_Island_Nagasaki_Japan.jpg.license.txt with: Source URL, License CC BY 2.0, Attribution text, Download date, Commercial use YES, SA required NO, Used in scenes S007. Add attribution to video description template. ALSO DOWNLOAD companion C007-B: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/cc/Cku-74-20_c45_6_hashima.jpg (one file, serves both S007 historical insert and S024 final shot).

decisions.source_title: Fourth mine pit hoistroom remains — Hashima 1992
decisions.attribution_text: Fourth mine pit hoistroom, Hashima Island (1992) by Gohachiyasu1214 / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY-SA 4.0
decisions.structure_mismatch_note: image_plan.json IMG007 references 'Building 30 Hashima' (30号棟 — residential concrete apartment block). C008-A shows the Fourth Mine Pit Hoistroom (第四竪坑捲座跡) — industrial coal shaft infrastructure, a different structure. Both are authentic Hashima buildings. HUMAN DECISION REQUIRED: Does S008 scene content specifically require Building 30, or is industrial mining infrastructure acceptable for this narrative beat? If Building 30 is required, C008-A is not the correct candidate.
decisions.sensitive_content_note: C008-A shows the hoistroom exterior/mechanical equipment shot 18 years after closure. NO people. NO forced labor context. This is NOT the mine tunnel interior (S011a/IMG009 — which has its own CRITICAL human review flag). Industrial archaeology photography.
decisions.risk_rationale: CC BY-SA 4.0. Same SA concern as C001-A. Color grade and motion effects constitute modifications triggering SA clause.
decisions.paid_alternative: C008-D (Getty Images) — 905 Gunkanjima images, Royalty-Free, commercial video use available. RECOMMENDED if CC BY-SA review is blocked. Search 'Gunkanjima industrial' or 'Hashima mine equipment'. Confirm license covers online commercial video at checkout.
decisions.exact_human_action: Step 1: Confirm whether S008 scene requires Building 30 specifically or whether industrial mine infrastructure (hoistroom) is acceptable for this beat. Check scene.json S008 scene_description. If hoistroom acceptable: proceed. If Building 30 required: search PIXTA/Getty for '30号棟 端島' instead. Step 2: Complete CC BY-SA legal review (same review covers C001-A, C008-A, C020-A — one review unlocks all three). If approved: download C008-A, rename to Hashima_mine_hoistroom_1992.jpg. If blocked: purchase from Getty Images under Royalty-Free (confirm online video commercial use explicitly at checkout).

decisions.attribution_text: Hashima Island ruins (2017) by Nsxbln / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY-SA 4.0
decisions.risk_rationale: CC BY-SA 4.0. Same SA concern as C001-A. Color grading to dark_documentary style at 6000x4000 resolution constitutes a significant derivative transformation.
decisions.fallback_notes: C020-C is the same Battle-Ship aerial as C007-A (CC BY 2.0, no SA). Visual match 7/10 for S020 — aerial framing is less suited for contemplative ACT IV tone. Creates a visual repeat with S007 if both use the same source. Use only as a last resort before paid stock.
decisions.paid_alternative: C020-D (PIXTA) — STRONGLY RECOMMENDED for S020 specifically. ACT IV (approach to Ma beat) is the emotional peak of the video. A real dusk or golden-hour photograph of Hashima ruins at this position will significantly outperform an aerial or a visual repeat. Search: 軍艦島 夕暮れ / 端島 夕焼け / Gunkanjima dusk / Hashima atmosphere. Filter horizontal orientation.
decisions.exact_human_action: Option A: Complete CC BY-SA legal review (same review covers C001-A, C008-A, C020-A simultaneously). If approved: download https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/8/88/Hashima_Island_ruins_1.jpg. Option B (recommended for best emotional impact): Purchase from PIXTA, search 軍艦島 夕暮れ or Gunkanjima dusk. Royalty-Free, no SA, no legal ambiguity. S020 is the last image before the 40-second Ma beat silence — it deserves the strongest real photography investment in this project.

decisions.upgrade_note: C024-A (Hashima_Island_2023.jpg, CC BY-SA 4.0, visual match 9/10) shares a single license review with C001-A. If CC BY-SA legal review is approved, upgrade S024 from C024-B to C024-A for higher visual quality and the sea-level 2023 perspective. One download covers both S001 and S024.
decisions.source_title: National Land Image Archive — Hashima aerial 1974 (year of closure)
decisions.license_type: Attribution (CC-BY equivalent — National Land Image Information license)
decisions.attribution_text: Aerial photograph of Hashima Island (1974) / Source: National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs) / Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism of Japan
decisions.attribution_note: Use exact text above verbatim. Do not paraphrase. Japanese government license requires specific attribution string.
decisions.crop_note: Portrait orientation (1900x2100). Crop to 16:9: approximately 1900x1069 centered. Sufficient resolution for 1920x1080 output. Apply slow_zoom_out motion to create contemplative closing feel.
decisions.editorial_concept: The video opens with the island seen from sea level in 2023 (S001). It closes with an aerial government photograph from 1974 — the year the island stopped. This time-loop (2023→1974) reinforces the video's core argument: Hashima is a place where time itself seems to have paused. Strongest editorial concept for the final frame.
decisions.shared_file_note: C024-B is the same file as C007-B (companion download for S007). Download once, use for both: S007 historical insert during ACT III narration + S024 final shot.
decisions.exact_human_action: IMMEDIATE — NO LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED. This is the same file as the C007-B companion download for S007. If S007 download is complete, this file already exists at assets/real_images/downloaded/S007/Cku-74-20_c45_6_hashima.jpg. Create a symlink or copy to assets/real_images/downloaded/S024/. If not yet downloaded: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/cc/Cku-74-20_c45_6_hashima.jpg. Crop to 16:9. Add exact attribution to video description (see attribution_text above — do not paraphrase).

scenario_a.label: Scenario A — Only S007 approved
scenario_a.notes: S007 replaced by C007-A. Four scenes remain AI. 80% > 75% limit.

scenario_b.label: Scenario B — S007 + all CC BY-SA approved
scenario_b.legal_prerequisite: Written legal clearance that CC BY-SA derivative works in a commercial YouTube documentary do not require the video to adopt CC BY-SA
scenario_b.notes: S007 (C007-A) + S001 (C001-A) + S008 (C008-A) + S020 (C020-A) + S024 (C024-A). Best outcome for visual quality AND compliance. One legal review unlocks four candidates.

scenario_c.label: Scenario C — S007 approved + remaining 4 via paid stock
scenario_c.cost_note: PIXTA and/or Getty license required. Cleanest commercial path with no legal ambiguity.
scenario_c.notes: Same AI% as Scenario B. Higher cost, zero SA risk. Recommended if legal review is unavailable.

scenario_d.label: Scenario D — Only S007 approved, remaining 4 stay AI fallback
scenario_d.notes: Same as Scenario A numerically. COMP-001 remains BLOCKER. Image generation cannot begin.

scenario_e_bonus.label: Scenario E (Bonus) — S007 + S024 both attribution-safe, no legal review
scenario_e_bonus.notes: S007 (C007-A, CC BY 2.0) + S024 (C024-B, Japan Govt 1974, attribution-only). Both immediately safe to download. 75.0% exactly meets the 75% contract limit. Zero buffer — any future AI addition re-triggers violation. RECOMMENDED as an immediate partial resolution while legal review or paid stock for S001/S008/S020 proceeds.
scenario_e_bonus.recommended_action: Execute Scenario E immediately (download C007-A and C024-B), then proceed toward Scenario B or C for final resolution with 15-point compliance buffer.
